message:
feat(mcp tui/system_health + atc summary): surface kernel-budget observed source in TUI and snapshot

Follow-up to the ATC tick-budget consolidation.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/tui_screens/system_health.rs
- New `atc_budget_observed(snapshot)` helper returns a
  `(source, observed_micros)` pair:
    * `("kernel", snapshot.budget.kernel_total_micros)` when the
      operator snapshot is live and kernel_total_micros > 0 (so the
      TUI prefers the true kernel time whenever available),
    * `("tick", snapshot.last_tick_duration_micros)` otherwise (no
      live summary).
- The Budget line in the System Health screen now prints
  `"<observed>us / <budget>us  p95≈<p95>us  source=<kernel|tick>"`
  so an operator reading the TUI can tell at a glance whether the
  number reflects the actual kernel runtime or the outer wall-clock
  fallback. p95 continues to use `atc_tick_p95_micros` since that
  histogram covers the outer loop — the distinction only matters for
  the "current sample" field.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/lib.rs
- Minor wiring adjustments to populate the `budget.source` /
  `budget.observed_micros` fields on the snapshot that feeds the
  TUI. Also keeps the JSON shape backwards-compatible: older
  consumers that don't know about the new fields continue to see
  the same duration numbers they always did.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
